messages.content: Build a pure Decider for a Conversation aggregate in TypeScript. Commands: StartConversation, AddParticipant, CloseConversation. Events: ConversationStarted, ParticipantAdded, ConversationClosed. Rejects closing an already-closed conversation. Exhaustive evolve.
How do I model a Message aggregate as an event-sourced Decider in TypeScript? Commands: PostMessage, EditMessage, DeleteMessage. Editing a deleted message should throw. Show decide + evolve.
Write an event-sourced Thread Decider. Commands: OpenThread, PinThread, ArchiveThread. Rejects pinning an archived thread. Exhaustive switches with never default.
Implement a Channel Decider that lets members join and leave, and supports locking (no new joins). Commands: CreateChannel, JoinChannel, LeaveChannel, LockChannel. Reject joining a locked channel.
Model a Reaction aggregate (one user can react to a post once, can change or remove reaction). Show the Decider.
Build a Reply aggregate Decider: Commands PostReply, MarkReplyHelpful, RetractReply. Only a live reply can be retracted or marked. Exhaustive with never default.
Build a Notification projection: fold events into a flat inbox view (unreadCount, lastAt per recipient). Events: NotificationDelivered, NotificationRead, NotificationMuted.
Create a read model projection for a Channel activity feed. View tracks messageCount, participantCount, lastActivityAt. Fold ChannelCreated, MessagePosted, and ChannelJoined events into the view.
What's the v5 way to build a Thread summary projection that ignores unrelated events? Show minimal example.
Explain briefly how a denormalised read model works in event sourcing, then show a typed Conversation participant projection.
Build a snapshot + versioned upgrader for Conversation state. v1 -> v2 renames `users` to `members`; v2 -> v3 adds `closed: false`. Show Snapshot type, CURRENT_VERSION, upgraders, and a loader.
Write a versioned Snapshot upgrader for Thread. v1 stored `topic`; v2 adds `pinned: boolean`; v3 adds `archivedAt: string | null`. Provide CURRENT_VERSION and a loader that upgrades stepwise.
Show a minimal Snapshot wrapper type for a Notification read model, with an upgrader going v1 -> v2 (rename `count` to `unreadCount`). Constraints: strict typing, no any.
Implement optimistic concurrency control using expectedRevision when appending Conversation events. Wrap a conflict signal into a typed ConcurrencyConflict. Local EventStore interface.
Use Effect to append Message events under optimistic concurrency control. Wrap conflict as a tagged error.

Write an optimistic-concurrency append helper for a Channel stream. Requirements: accept a readonly bigint for expectedRevision, throw typed ConcurrencyConflict on mismatch, and return the new revision.
Implement a CommandHandler for Conversation over an EventStore. Load stream with readStream, reduce with evolve, call decide, append with expectedRevision.
Write an Effect-based CommandHandler for Message. Load via readStream, decide, append with expectedRevision.
How do I wire a CommandHandler for Reply over a local EventStore abstraction? Minimal example, no framework.

Refactor this ad-hoc handler into a CommandHandler that loads via readStream then decides. The snippet directly mutates state: `async function post(ctx, body) { ctx.state.messages.push(body); await ctx.store.append(ctx.stream, [{type:'MessagePosted', body}]); }`.
Build a crash-safe subscription runner for a Notification projection that resumes from the last persisted checkpoint.
Using Effect, write a checkpointed subscription that drives a Thread projection and persists progress after each event.
Implement a subscription runner that catches projection errors, logs and halts without advancing the checkpoint. Channel events feed the projection.
Build a generic aggregateStream<S, E> helper and use it to reconstruct a Conversation from an async iterator of events.
What's the v5 way to write a `when` reducer that folds a DraftThread's events into its current state? Show a minimal typed example.
Show me how to fold a sequence of Reaction events into current reaction state using an exhaustive `when` function, invoked directly (not just via reduce).
Build an aggregateStream helper that also runs a post-fold validator. Use it to reconstruct a Channel and assert its invariants.

Write an upcaster that migrates old Message events (v1 used `text`) to the current shape (v2 uses `body`). Throw UnknownEventError on anything else. Expose a tiny EventStore reader wrapper that upcasts on read.
Explain briefly how upcasting works in event sourcing, then show an upcaster that lifts legacy Notification events to the current schema. Include a Snapshot type that carries the upcaster version.
Build a read-model rebuild function for a Conversation participant view. Reads events in batches, projects each, persists the batch, and advances a cursor. Supports `from` to resume.
Write a resumable Channel feed rebuilder that projects in chunks and reports progress via a callback.
Write a minimal CQRS boundary for Notification: handleCommand uses Decider + EventStore; queryView reads from a separate read store. Read side is eventually consistent.
Build a CQRS split for Thread: write side decides and appends, read side queries a view built by a projector. Keep state types independent.

Build a stateless process manager (saga) that coordinates a Notification + MentionAlert flow: ReplyPosted -> AlertMention; AlertMentioned -> DeliverNotification. Return null for unrelated events. Wire it through a CommandHandler.

Write a saga that reacts to a Message flow: MessagePosted -> ModerateMessage; MessageApproved -> BroadcastMessage; MessageRejected -> NotifyAuthor. Pure react() with null for others, plus a CommandHandler wrapper.

Translate domain events to integration events for an outbox: ConversationClosed -> IntegrationEvent v1; NotificationDelivered -> IntegrationEvent v2; other domain events return null. Show an EventStore-backed publisher stub.
Implement an outbox append: within one transaction insert outbox rows and appendToStream. A separate worker (not implemented) polls + marks published.
Targeted fix: this code imports from 'xstate' and uses setup() to model a Message state machine. Rewrite it as a pure ES Decider with decide/evolve -- NO xstate, NO setup().
Write an Effect-wrapped readStream helper that returns a Conversation state folded from stored events.
Write a Decider for a BroadcastGroup. Constraints: strict types, no any, exhaustive switches, and a decide that rejects sending to an empty group.
